_meta.purpose: Evidence + engine config for Crosssight (plan.md §3.2, §13.2–§13.4; ARCHITECTURE.md §3.3, §4.1).
_meta.status: VERIFIED against datasets/system/system_climate_relationships.csv (System research graph, 2026-06-20). effectSize = median_effect_size, numStudies = num_studies, highestCited = highest_cited, sourceRowId = relationship_id — all read directly from the graph. Every canonical + provenance row exists with these values; nothing is invented (plan.md §13.2).
rules: The engine SUMS ONLY `canonical[]` — one non-overlapping term per (pollutant → outcome). This is the double-counting fix (plan.md §13.2).
`provenanceOnly[]` rows NEVER enter the arithmetic. They are display-only citation chips for F11 (e.g. the headline '66 studies').
effectSize is a DIMENSIONLESS RELATIVE WEIGHT, not a relative risk or beta. The engine claims relative ordering, never an absolute admissions count (plan.md §13.1–§13.2).

canonical.label: Nitrogen dioxide → asthma
canonical.gateNote: ONE NO2→asthma term per catchment. Applied ONLY to catchments with roadside=false. For roadside=true catchments, roadside_asthma REPLACES this term (not additive) — the two are mutually exclusive (resolves S9).

canonical.label: Traffic-related pollution → asthma
canonical.gateNote: ONE NO2→asthma term per catchment. Applied ONLY to catchments with roadside=true, where it REPLACES no2_asthma (not additive) — the two are mutually exclusive. Non-roadside catchments use no2_asthma instead (resolves S9).

canonical.label: Extreme heat → mortality (heat-stress proxy)
canonical.honestyNote: Uses an extreme-heat→mortality effect as a PROXY for acute heat burden on respiratory-vulnerable populations; outcome differs from respiratory admissions — surfaced transparently in F11.
canonical.gateNote: Contributes 0 below heatGate.startC, ramps to full at heatGate.fullC; UKHSA alert applies ukhsaBoost (plan.md §13.3).

provenanceOnly.label: Air pollution → cardiovascular disease
provenanceOnly.excludedBecause: secondary cardiac sub-index; off by default (plan.md §13.2)

exposureNormalization._note: Normalize each exposure to 0–1 against ABSOLUTE health thresholds, not live min/max (plan.md §13.3). Linear, clamp >1.

o3.note: Shown in F3 toggle; NOT summed unless a coherent O3 term is confirmed in the graph.

heatGate.note: J-shaped: heat term is 0 below startC.

lagKernel._note: Days-offset weights convolved with the exposure series to build the F12 pressure curve (plan.md §13.4). Effects peak ~1–3 days out, fade by ~day 7. Sums ≈ 1. Tune to literature.

bands._note: Absolute thresholds on the 0–100 RPI so colours are stable across refreshes/screenshots (plan.md §13.1).

vulnerabilityWeight._note: Normalized 0.5–1.5 multiplier so vulnerability modulates rather than dominates the index. Per-hospital values live in catchments.json.

demandModel.note: projectedDemand = illustrativeDemandBaseline.value × (1 + demandSensitivity × RPI/100); headroom = capacity.value − projectedDemand (resolves C6)

rpiCalibration.method: Absolute reference rawScore at all canonical exposures = 0.6 and vulnerabilityWeight = 1.25, INCLUDING the roadside term (roadside_asthma replaces no2_asthma per D5) and the heat term. rawScore = Σ over canonical terms ( exposure × effectSize × vulnerabilityWeight ).
rpiCalibration.note: RPI = 100 × clamp(rawScore / referenceRaw, 0, 1). Absolute reference, NOT live min-max. Arithmetic with VERIFIED System-graph effect sizes: referenceRaw = 0.6 × 1.25 × (pm25_respiratory + roadside_asthma + heat_mortality) = 0.6 × 1.25 × (0.06 + 0.2958 + 0.3467) = 0.75 × 0.7025 = 0.526875.
